articles.title: How Reading Slowly Filled the Gaps I Didn't Know I Had
articles.excerpt: A personal reflection on how a simple reading habit became a quiet anchor at the end of each day.

author.name: Anonymous
author.bio: A reader finding stillness between pages.

articles.title: The Three Essential Properties of the Engineering Mindset
articles.excerpt: Engineers use a unique mode of thinking based on seeing everything as a system. Adopting an engineering mindset can help you in any field.

articles.title: Why First Principles Thinking Beats Analogical Reasoning
articles.excerpt: Most people reason by analogy. First principles thinking breaks problems down to their fundamental truths and builds up from there.

articles.title: The Art of Making Decisions That Stick
articles.excerpt: Most decisions fail not because they were wrong, but because they lacked commitment.

articles.title: Inversion: The Problem-Solving Technique Nobody Uses
articles.excerpt: Instead of asking how to achieve success, ask how to guarantee failure. Then avoid those things.
